confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_75d1eabe-e310-46c2-8059-f75fee63d2fd read back as received

payload.authority_note: Operator identified this as a responsibility hole and requested submission.

incident.correction_taken: Architect sent Bob a note with exact blocking evidence and asked him to continue task 58: fix the test/parameter contract, verify cases, commit, and report for review.
incident.root_cause: The system lacks a first-class path from informal builder completion claim to architect reconciliation. Builder claim, git state, test state, and task lifecycle state can diverge without a compact governed handoff.
incident.what_happened: Operator reported Bob believed task 58 was finished. Architect inspected task state and found implementation files present, but task 58 remained claimed, had unchecked criteria, no report, uncommitted changes, and Test-CurrentOperatorSurfaceIdentity.ps1 failed.

payload.proposed_invariant: agent completion claim -> architect reconciliation -> review if in_review, or return-to-builder with exact blocking evidence

requests.text: Add an architect-loop duty: any builder says done/completed claim must trigger reconciliation against task lifecycle status, report presence, criteria, tests/evidence, and git commit state before being accepted as review-ready.

Add a compact command such as narada task reconcile-claim <task|agent> that reports lifecycle status, report presence, checked criteria, dirty files, latest commits, and recommended action: review, return-to-builder, or ask clarification.
Extend builder role-loop guidance so builders finish through task report/finish, not only chat claims, and receive a clear repair when they believe they finished but lifecycle state is still claimed.

Document that chat/operator-surface completion claims are observations, not lifecycle truth. Durable task lifecycle remains authority, and Architect owns reconciliation across claim, evidence, and state.

payload.summary: A builder believed task 58 was finished, but durable task state still showed claimed, criteria unchecked, no report, uncommitted files, and a failing test. Architect did not initially treat the claim as a reconciliation trigger. Narada needs an explicit completion-claim reconciliation duty.
payload.title: CAPA: reconcile builder completion claims against task lifecycle before review
